SEARCH
Package server.scheduler

Interface SchedulerJob

All Known Implementing Classes:
ConfirmTimeoutJob, NoShowJob, ReminderJob, WaitlistGrabExpiryJob
public interface SchedulerJob
One timed maintenance task driven by SchedulerService. A job is plain, synchronous, idempotent-by-intent code: the service handles the threading, the fixed-interval polling, exception isolation, and manual "run now" triggers, so an implementation only describes what to do, not when.

A job must catch nothing for control flow — uncaught exceptions are contained by the service so one failing run never kills the shared executor — and should log a one-line summary only when it actually acts, keeping the server console quiet on idle polls.

Force mode. Every job runs in one of two modes. The scheduled timer calls runOnce(false) — the normal sweep, which only touches rows past the job's real time threshold (24h/2h/1h). A manual "run now" from the server console calls runOnce(true), which drops the time predicate so the job acts on every eligible row immediately — letting the live defense produce each effect on demand without shrinking .env or waiting for a window to lapse.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    name()
    Stable identifier for this job, used as the registry key and the handle the server console passes to SchedulerService.runNow(String).
    default void
    runOnce()
    Convenience for the normal, non-forced sweep — equivalent to runOnce(false).
    void
    runOnce(boolean force)
    Performs one sweep.

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()
      Stable identifier for this job, used as the registry key and the handle the server console passes to SchedulerService.runNow(String).
      Returns:
      the job's unique name

    • runOnce

      void runOnce(boolean force)
      Performs one sweep. Invoked on a scheduler thread, every poll interval and on each manual trigger.
      Parameters:
      force - when true, drop the job's time threshold and act on every eligible row now (the manual "run now" path); when false, the normal scheduled sweep that respects the real 24h/2h/1h window

    • runOnce

      default void runOnce()
      Convenience for the normal, non-forced sweep — equivalent to runOnce(false).
